title: Sentinel Shield rollback / superseding-release advisory
description: The machine-readable record emitted by `authorize-production-release.sh rollback-advisory` and `declare-superseded`. It NEVER represents a tag deletion or a tag move: a Sentinel Shield rollback publishes a SUPERSEDING fixed release, marks the affected versions, and gives consumers explicit rollback/upgrade guidance. Released tags are immutable — an advisory records the remediation, it does not mutate history. Contains NO secrets.

kind.description: 'superseded' when a fixed superseding release replaces an affected one; 'rollback' when the advisory recommends consumers move back to a known-good prior version pending a fix.

reason.description: Why the affected versions are being superseded / rolled back.

affected_versions.description: The released versions this advisory marks as affected. Their tags are NOT deleted or moved.

status.description: 'superseded' — replaced by superseding_version; 'yanked-advisory' — advised against but still present (never actually deleted); 'affected' — impacted, remediation pending.

superseding_version.description: The fixed release that replaces the affected versions (required when kind == 'superseded').

rollback_to.description: Known-good prior version consumers should pin to (used when kind == 'rollback').

action.description: The remediation action. NEVER 'delete-tag' or 'move-tag' — those are refused by the tool.

consumer_guidance.description: Ordered, copy-pasteable steps for consumers (e.g. how to upgrade to the superseding release or pin to the rollback target).

references.description: Links to the incident, the superseding release notes, and the security advisory.
